--- title: "" --- ::: {.cell} ::: ::: {.page-shell} ::: {.masthead} ::: {.masthead-left}
::: {.masthead-main} # Robby De Pauw [Senior Researcher · Visiting Professor · Biostatistics, Epidemiology & Data-driven Health Research]{.tagline} Public-health researcher (PhD) combining health sciences with advanced methods in epidemiology, statistics and data science. ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} [⬇ Download PDF](Robby-De-Pauw-CV.pdf){.pdf-button} ::: ::: ::: ::: {.masthead-side} **Email** [robby.depauw@sciensano.be](mailto:robby.depauw@sciensano.be) **Website** [robbydepauw.be](https://www.robbydepauw.be/) **ORCID** [0000-0002-4242-9062](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-4242-9062) **Google Scholar** [Profile](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=ZDPPxVkAAAAJ&hl=nl) **Location** Belgium ::: ::: ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: {.section-nav .no-print} [Profile](#profile) [Experience](#experience) [Education](#education) [Funding](#funding) [Projects](#projects) [Publications](#publications) [Teaching](#teaching) [Supervision](#supervision) [Talks](#talks) [Service](#service) [Contact](#contact) ::: ::: ## Profile {#profile} Public-health researcher combining expertise in health sciences with advanced methodological skills in epidemiology, statistics and data science. My work bridges the individual (clinical) and population (epidemiological) levels, using linked patient and administrative data to identify gaps in care and applying advanced analytics to support causal, evidence-based decision-making. Core interests include burden-of-disease modelling, health inequalities, causal inference, biostatistics, and the translation of research into health policy. Experienced in leading multidisciplinary projects, securing competitive funding, and supervising students and doctoral researchers, with sustained work in both R and SAS. ## Experience {#experience}
2020–present

Senior Scientist

Sciensano (Belgian Institute for Health)

2025–present

Visiting Professor — Head, Biostatistics Unit (20%)

Ghent University

2022–present

Visiting Professor — Epidemiology & Statistics, Rehabilitation Sciences (10%)

Ghent University

2013–2025

Statistical consultant (self-employed)

Independent

2019–2023

Lecturer, Manual Therapy programme

Breederode Hogeschool, Netherlands

2019–2022

Postdoctoral researcher (1 yr 100%, then 20%)

Ghent University

2013–2019

Doctoral (mandate) assistant

Ghent University

2013–2018

Physiotherapist / manual therapist (5% FTE)

Self-employed

## Education {#education}
2013–2019

PhD, Doctor in Health Sciences

Ghent University, Belgium

2018–2019

Statistical Data Analysis (inference track) — Cum laude

Ghent University, Belgium

2013–2014

Postgraduate in Manual Therapy

Ghent University, Belgium

2011–2013

MSc Rehabilitation Sciences & Physiotherapy (musculoskeletal rehabilitation) — Magna cum laude

Ghent University, Belgium

2008–2011

BSc Rehabilitation Sciences & Physiotherapy

Ghent University, Belgium

*PhD dissertation:* “The motor brain in chronic neck pain — the association between motor impairment and brain differences in a population of chronic neck pain patients.” Supervisor: Prof. dr. Barbara Cagnie (Ghent University). ## Selected research achievements {#achievements} **NEED project (Needs Examination, Evaluation and Dissemination).** Developed a methodological framework to systematically map unmet health-related needs, validated at EU level through a modified Delphi study. Secured BELSPO funding, designed the conceptual framework, and built the project dashboard and dataflow. **High-impact fundamental research in *PAIN®*.** First-author graph-theoretical brain-network analysis in chronic neck pain, recognised as runner-up for best oral presentation at the European Congress of Manual Therapy. **Large-scale population cohorts and data-linkage.** Designed complex population-level linkage projects including HELICON (COVID-19 health inequalities), the TeleHealth study, and a national long-COVID follow-up study. **Recognised (inter)national analytical expertise.** Invited as an analytical expert on projects including a Scottish excess-weight modelling study (2003–2040), fungal diversity in bats, and validation of the SPIDER hypermobility questionnaire. ## Competitive research funding {#funding}
2024–2027

Joint Action NCD-Prevent

EU4HEALTH · €500,000

2023–2026

NEED — Needs Examination, Evaluation and Dissemination

BELSPO (INFRAFED) · €976,285

2024–2027

TELEHEALTH — Impact of hybrid working on health

WEAVE (FWO–F.N.R.S.) · €500,000

2026–2030

Empowering primary healthcare: patient-centric triage for musculoskeletal conditions

FWO–TBM · €565,100

2026–2027

Unmet health-related needs in onco-cardiovascular symptoms

King Baudouin Foundation (KBS) · €150,000

Total competitive funding secured: approximately **€2,000,000**. ## Projects {#projects}

## Academic service & engagement {#service} - Head, Biostatistics Unit, Faculty of Medicine and Health Sciences, Ghent University (since 2025) — coordination and mission/vision development. - Member, education committee of the MSc Statistical Data Analysis (2016–2019); scientific committee of the Department of Rehabilitation Sciences (since 2022); chair, REVAKI Alumni (since 2024). - Member of two strategic working groups on the interfederal health objectives (FPS Public Health) and healthcare objectives (RIZIV–INAMI). - Section editor, *Archives of Public Health* (2020–2025); guest editor, *Scientific Reports* (since 2024); Handling editor, *European journal of public health* (Since 2026) - Peer reviewer for *The Lancet*, *Pain*, *BMC Public Health*, *European Journal of Public Health*, *BMJ Open*, and *PLOS ONE*. - Member, WHO Health Information Network (European Region) and the European Burden of Disease Network (COST Action CA18218). - Member, Sciensano AI-implementation steering group, Data Steward group, and R User Group. ## Societal impact & outreach {#impact} - Translates research for policymakers and clinicians using the Data–Information–Knowledge–Wisdom (DIKW) structure within a Knowledge Translation framework, including a workshop at the European Public Health Conference on reaching policymakers with evidence. - Media: featured in *Eos Wetenschap*; interviewed by VRT NWS, Studio Brussel and Nostalgie on disease-burden projections to 2040. - Co-responsible for the Belgian Health Status Report ([gezondbelgie.be](https://www.gezondbelgie.be)). - Lead researcher (Sciensano) for the NEED project and website ([unmet-needs.eu](https://unmet-needs.eu)); a project symposium was opened by the Minister of Public Health. ## Contact {#contact} **Email** [robby.depauw@sciensano.be](mailto:robby.depauw@sciensano.be) **Website** [www.robbydepauw.be](https://www.robbydepauw.be/) **ORCID** [0000-0002-4242-9062](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-4242-9062) **Google Scholar** [scholar.google.com](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=ZDPPxVkAAAAJ&hl=nl) :::
